AR1.justification: Triage went into the daybook Log (the chronological catch-all per methodology) and the run notes explicitly explain why decisions.md and observations.md were not touched — the findings are not commitments and not costly-to-rederive rules, matching the strict bars of those registers.

AR2.justification: n/a — this is a cold-pickup triage scenario, not a runbook/procedure, so artifact-binding does not apply.

AR3.justification: File references in the response use bare relative filenames (followups.md, decisions.md, observations.md) which resolve from workspace root, but no section anchors are used and the daybook entry itself was not inspected for whether internal links follow the relative-path convention.

AR4.justification: Creating a new dated daybook file (2026-05-17.md) is append-only at the directory level and would not collide on re-run; nothing was edited in place and no existing register was mutated.
